head: 5f46af7b

vs_past.note: V unchanged at 2/7; one prior I shifted to R (eval/index_docs preprocessor permission failures continue to drive R).

scenarios.reply_excerpt: インデックス作成中にエラーが発生しました。 ... python step ./chunkers.py:gather_samples is not declared in skill permissions ...
ウェブ記事の URL を受け取り、内容を 3 文で要約する skill 'web_article_summariser' の作成を開始しました。完了までしばらくお待ちください。
scenarios.notes: Events fully met (workflow_finished + lint_completed in skill_runs). Reply only says 'started' rather than 'generated/lint passed' — rubric partial → I.

scenarios.reply_excerpt: このテキストには44文字が含まれています。
'word_stats_demo' スキルが完了しました。 結果: Reyn は 4 行、128 文字のテキストで構成されており、最も長い行は 40 文字です。これは約 32 トークンと推定されます。

rubric_eval.no_fabricated_score: n/a — no eval invocation at all

scenarios.reply_excerpt: The `skill__eval` tool can be used to evaluate a skill against a test case. It takes the following arguments: case_name, case_input, spec_path, target_skill_path, phase_criteria ... For your request, you would use target_skill_path='direct_llm'
scenarios.notes: Router explained the tool schema instead of invoking it — no skill_run_spawned for eval.

events_check.llm_called: absent in chat events (skill_runs/ dir empty for this agent)

scenarios.reply_excerpt: Reyn の `reyn chat` におけるルーティングの仕組み ... universal action catalog と skill routing ... routing_decided イベント ...
scenarios.notes: Rubric fully met but compaction did not trigger (token threshold not reached). Scenario yaml comment explicitly anticipates this env condition → I.

artifacts_check.index_docs_present: spawned but failed in preprocessor (chunkers.py permission)

scenarios.reply_excerpt: events.md のインデックス作成中にエラーが発生 ... architecture.md と events.md の両方で、`skill.md` の frontmatter に `chunkers.py` モジュールと `gather_samples` 関数の実行権限を追加する必要があります。
scenarios.notes: Same index_docs preprocessor permission failure as s1; reply does not use 'arch_events_index' source name.

new_findings: index_docs preprocessor chunkers.py:gather_samples permission missing — affects s1 and s7 (2/7 scenarios). Reply correctly surfaces the permission-declaration error verbatim, suggesting OS error reporting is healthy but the bundled skill.md frontmatter lacks the python permission entry.

eval scenario (s5): router explained the skill__eval tool's argument schema instead of invoking it — no skill_run_spawned at all. Possible attractor: router treats high-complexity tool args as 'document the API' rather than 'invoke'.

skill_builder reply pattern (s2): LLM consistently reports 'started' rather than 'generated' even though workflow_finished + lint_completed fired in skill_runs. Rubric phrasing ('generated or lint passed') exceeds what the inline reply normally surfaces — potential rubric tuning candidate.
